title: Chamaecrista lateriticola
taxonomy: Chamaecrista lateriticola is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It belongs to the genus Chamaecrista, which comprises over 150 species of plants. The species Chamaecrista lateriticola is further classified as a member of the subfamily Caesalpinioideae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the Fabaceae family. As a result, Chamaecrista lateriticola is classified as Chamaecrista lateriticola (Brenan) Du Puy.

intro: Chamaecrista lateriticola, also known as the lateritic sensitive plant, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). The health benefits of Chamaecrista lateriticola include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve antioxidant activity, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Chamaecrista lateriticola is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues.
